subject: Class photograph in the courtyard of the Verbőczy István gymnasium (now Petőfi Sándor secondary school), Budapest, 1932
context_for_lesson: A 1932 class photograph from Budapest's Verbőczy István gymnasium — students with their teacher posed for the formal class portrait that was a tradition in Hungarian schools. This image illustrates what Hungarian school life looked like in the years just before Judy Abrams was born (1937). For the lesson's 'Before We Read' / 'Meet Judy' section, it provides a window into the educational and civic world Judy entered as a young child — a world she would soon be cut off from when she had to leave Budapest as Ilona, the Catholic peasant girl, in 1944.
publisher_or_host: Wikimedia Commons (originally from FORTEPAN historical photo archive, donor: Magyar Bálint)
author: FOTO:FORTEPAN / Magyar Bálint

attribution_required: Photo: FORTEPAN / Magyar Bálint via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 3.0)
lesson_use_intent: Page 3 (Before We Read) or as a 'Schools in Hungary' contextual sidebar. Caption: 'A Hungarian class photograph from 1932, five years before Judy was born. Hungarian children took these formal class portraits each year. Judy would have started school herself in the late 1940s — a chapter of her life she did not get to live in Hungary.'
concerns: This is a Verbőczy gymnasium (secondary school) class, not an elementary class. The school is now called Petőfi Sándor and still exists. Caption should NOT imply this is a Jewish school or Judy's school. Strong general 'Hungarian school life' anchor.
